conbody.p: Overview: Encounter Details report helps CDI Manager to analyze the details around the encounters and work with the CDSs to take appropriate action.

The report provides answers to the following questions:
Which CDSs are not following the reconciliation best practices?
Which CDS could benefit from additional education on the Reconciliation process?
How do we find the gaps in the reconciliation process?
Which cases were incorrectly reconciled?
What is the reimbursement impact the CDS had on a case?
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE One CDI Operations Encounter Details .

Encounter Details displays the following:
KPI Card: Total Encounters
Encounter and Review Details
On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets generated in the table.

row.entry: The total count of encounters with all statuses including Review Not Needed for the reporting period. When the Admit Date is applied, then this card displays the count of admitted encounters with all statuses including Review Not Needed. When the Discharge Date is applied, then this card displays the count of discharged encounters with all statuses including Review Not Needed.

p.b: Encounter Details - Encounter and Review Details

conbody.p: Info icon(text on hover): By default, all encounter statuses are considered except Review Not Needed. Select 'Review Not Needed' options from the 'Encounter Status' filter to analyze the Review Not Needed encounters.

row.entry: Condition when the Value is blank
The facility assigned to the encounter in the format Facility Description (Facility ID).
The Account Number assigned to the encounter.
The MRN assigned to the encounter.
The name of the patient associated with the encounter. Patient name is displayed in the format : Last Name, First Name

entry.p: The Admission Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.

p.b: Data in the report is sorted by the Admission Date in Descending order.

entry.p: The Discharge Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a discharge date assigned.

row.entry: The length of stay in days for each encounter. LOS value is displayed in blue font, when LOS is greater than GMLOS for the encounter.

entry.p: LOS = Discharge Date/Time – Admit Date/Time
OR
LOS = Last Data Refresh Date/Time – Admit Date/Time
The date and time when the initial review was performed on the encounter.
The date must be in the Facility's time-zone.

p.note: If the Initial Review Date/time is after the Discharge Date/Time, Initial Review Date/Time is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have an initial review date.
The name of the CDS who performed the initial review.
Format: Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The Tracking Codes assigned to the encounter in comma separated format. You can view a maximum of 10 tracking codes for an encounter.
Example: Quality Review, Prov Education
The rank assigned to the encounter prior to the Initial Review.
Example: 1, 2, etc.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Rank at Initial Review' assigned.

row.entry:
Displays the number of hours when the encounter status is set as Review Complete Date/Time and when the working review was first created for the selected time period. The date must be in the Facility's time-zone.
Example: 05/24/2020 10:10:00 AM ET.
Review TAT (Hours) for each encounter = Review Complete (Date/Time stamp) – Initial Review (Date/Time stamp).

entry.p: The 'primary' Working DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.

row.entry: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'

entry.p: The WT associated with the 'primary' Working DRG displayed on the Review page.
Example: 1.0393
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'SOI' from the working review.

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'ROM' from the working review.

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'AMLOS' from the working review.

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'GMLOS ' from the working review.

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'primary' Possible DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.

p.note: This column displays the ‘Working DRG’ value in the orange font for the encounters that have a ‘Working DRG’ assigned the ‘Possible DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The WT associated with the 'primary' Possible DRG displayed on the Review page.
Example: 1.0393

p.note: This column displays the ‘Working DRG WT’ value in the orange font for the encounters that have a ‘Working DRG’ assigned the ‘Possible DRG’ is blank or null. Example: 1.0393

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Code and description of the Reconciliation Assessment DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)
The WT associated with the Reconciliation Assessment DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393
The 'SOI' from the Reconciliation Assessment.

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'ROM' from the Reconciliation Assessment.

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'AMLOS' from the Reconciliation Assessment.

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'GMLOS' from the Reconciliation Assessment.

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The Code and description of the Final Coded DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)
Note:
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The WT associated with the Final Coded DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The Final Coded. 'SOI'.

p.note: Advanced SOI Values are used if Primary SOI values are blank.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The Final Coded. 'ROM'.

p.note: Advanced ROM Values are used if Primary ROM values are blank.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The Final Coded 'AMLOS'.

p.note: Advanced AMLOS Values are used if Primary AMLOS values are blank.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.
The Final Coded 'GMLOS'.

p.note: Advanced GMLOS Values are used if Primary GMLOS values are blank.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.
The shift in the WT at the encounter level.

p.note: Variance can be negative, positive, or zero. Negative ‘Variance’ is displayed in red font E.g. - 1.0178.

entry.p: Rounded to 4 decimal places. Example: 1.0178
Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (or Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank)
For encounters with blank Final DRG WT, Variance = Possible DRG WT - Working DRG WT ( If Possible DRG/WT is blank, Working DRG WT is considered. In this case Variance will always br zero).

ul.li: Variance is not calculated for the encounters that do not have an initial review date.
Review Not Needed Encounters are not considered.

entry.p: The Blended Rate associated with the payer/Grouper assigned to the encounter.
When the Blended Rate is not available, the Operating Federal rate is displayed.
Example: $ 5,234
The ‘Reimbursement Impact’ value for the encounter.

ul.li: Rounded to 0 decimal places. Example: $ 5,234
Negative ‘Reimbursement Impact ($)’ is displayed in the red font.

row.entry: Reimbursement Impact = Variance x Blended Rate

entry.p: The Impact Type assigned to the encounter

ul.li: Financial Impact
No Impact
Both Impact
Unassigned Impact

entry.p: The Encounter Status assigned to the encounter.
entry.note: Encounters with the Status 'Review Not Needed' are not displayed by default. You can select 'Review Not Needed' options from the 'Encounter Status' filter to analyze the Review Not Needed encounters .

row.entry:
The CDS assigned to the encounter.
Format: Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID)

The Payer assigned to the encounter.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID)
The Grouper version associated with the payer assigned to the encounter.
The Unit assigned to the encounter.
The Patient Type assigned to the encounter.
The Visit Type assigned to the encounter.

entry.p: If the encounter is a pediatric encounter, it is displayed here.
Pediatric Age is configurable in CDE One application.

row.entry:
The value of the "Weekend Only Stay" applicable to the encounter. Weekend Only Stay = Y ( Unassigned encounters admitted after Friday, 6PM and Discharged before Sunday, 11.59 PM)
Weekend Only Stay = N (If the above criteria does not apply)

The Discharge Status assigned to the encounter.
Example: 01- 01 Self Care

ul.li: Displays the admit date range here. You can select required dates from the date lookup.
Default: Last 1 calendar months date range selected by default.
Displays the discharge date range here. You can select required dates from the date lookup.
With the Reset button, you can reset the admit date range.
Tooltip: Use "Ctrl + Click" to reset the both Admit Date and Discharge Date.
Displays a list of active and inactive facilities that you have access to. The list is sorted alphabetically.
You can select multiple facilities
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID)
Default: ALL
Displays a list of active and inactive payers associated with the facilities that you have access to. The list is sorted alphabetically.
You can select multiple payers
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID)
Default: ALL
Displays a list of active and inactive CDSs associated with the facilities you have access to.
The CDS Filter in all the reports displays the users with at-least CDS or CDS Manager role assigned.
"Unassigned CDS" displays data for the encounters that do not have a CDS assigned.
Format: Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID)
Default: ALL

Displays the available patient types.
Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient
Displays the list of available Visit Types.
You can select multiple Visit Types.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL
Displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL
Displays Weekend Only Stay values (Y/N).
You can search the Weekend Only Stay.
Default: ALL
Displays the list of available Encounter Statuses.
You can select multiple Encounter Statuses.
You can search the Encounter Status.
Default: Review Not Needed unchecked by default.
Displays the list of available Admit Services.
You can select multiple Admit Services.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL

row.entry: Displays Initial Review Date/time in red font when the Initial Review Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time.

entry.p: P-DRG: Displays the ‘Working DRG’ value in orange font for the reconciled encounters with blank ‘Possible DRG’.
P-DRG WT: Displays the ‘Working DRG WT’ value in orange font for the reconciled encounters with blank ‘Possible DRG’.

row.entry: Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank); Displays negative variance value in red font.
Configured at payer level; When Blended Rate is not available, Operating Federal rate is displayed.

Reimbursement Impact = (Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank)) x Blended Rate. E.g. $5321; Displays negative Reimbursement Impact in red font.
Advanced DRG SOI/ROM value is displayed when Primary DRG SOI/ROM is blank.
Advanced DRG AMLOS/GMLOS value is displayed when Primary DRG AMLOS/GMLOS is blank.
Financial Impact Clarifications: Clarifications with standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration.
Financial Impact Clarifications Agreed: Financial Impact Clarifications with standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ with Final response as Agreed..
LOS: Displays Length of Stay in blue font if LOS value greater than F-GMLOS; W-GMLOS value is considered in case of blank F-GMLOS.

Displays the number of hours when the encounter status is set as Review Complete Date/Time and when the working review was first created for the selected time period. The date must be in the Facility's time-zone. Example: 05/24/2020 10:10:00 AM ET.
Number of hours between Review Complete Date/Time and when the Working Review was first created for the selected time period. Review TAT (Hours) for each encounter = ‘Review Complete’ (Date/Time stamp) – Initial Review (Date/Time stamp).
